name: Vesna, Syrian conflict
description:
TW:Gore, rape

Escalation of the Syrian Conflict: November 2015

In November 2015, following the incident where a Turkish F-16 shot down a Russian Su-24M over Syria, relations between Turkey and Russia severely deteriorated. This event, occurring amidst existing tensions surrounding Turkey's alleged support for the Free Syrian Army (FSA) and other opposition groups, became the catalyst for a new, more intense phase of the conflict.

Russian escalation

In response to Turkey's actions, Moscow began to significantly increase its military presence in Syria. Additional troops and equipment were deployed to the Hmeymim airbase and the Tartus naval base. Concurrently, Russia substantially ramped up arms supplies to the Syrian Arab Army (SAA) and pro-government Alawite and Shia militias, who were actively engaged in combat against FSA formations.

Turkish and NATO response

As a countermeasure, Turkey initiated an overt military operation in northern Syria. Turkish forces advanced rapidly, taking control of Aleppo and displacing forces loyal to Assad, Kurdish formations, and ISIS units. Russia immediately reacted to the Turkish intervention, launching a counter-offensive against pro-Turkish militias and directly against the Turkish armed forces.

In the face of direct military confrontation with Russia, Turkey invoked Article 5 of the North Atlantic Treaty, seeking assistance from NATO member states. The United States and the United Kingdom expressed their readiness to support their ally. In response to Russian aggression, the U.S. and U.K. also decided to deploy their own troops to Syrian territory, leading to a further escalation of the conflict between major global powers.
